title: Implement named journey schema and editor

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.name: Missing named-journey builder
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/journey-authoring.test.ts --project property tests/property/journey-authoring.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: Three positive examples failed because buildJourneyVersion did not exist; six invalid-shape examples already rejected before implementation

commands.name: Missing journey service repository
commands.expectedFailure: All eight initial service scenarios failed because the in-memory journey repository did not exist

commands.name: Missing PostgreSQL journey repository
commands.expectedFailure: The PostgreSQL persistence scenario could not construct the not-yet-implemented repository

commands.name: Missing journey migration inventory entry
commands.expectedFailure: The migration inventory expected 0009 while the loader still returned only 0001 through 0008

commands.name: Missing signed journey route
commands.expectedFailure: The route suite could not import the not-yet-created signed journey endpoint

commands.name: Undefined named-journey browser behavior
commands.expectedFailure: All thirty-six prerequisite setup steps passed while three JRN-02 scenarios and twenty journey-editor step instances were undefined

commands.name: Agreement history scope regression
commands.expectedFailure: The service returned a journey from another agreement version because the validated agreement scope was not applied to repository results

commands.name: Review-due authorization regression
commands.expectedFailure: A journey was labeled RUNNABLE after the authorization's required human review time had passed

commands.name: Database action-scope complement regression
commands.expectedFailure: The PostgreSQL repository accepted UPLOAD outside the human authorization because the migration guard did not yet compare action arrays

commands.name: Current authorization and PostgreSQL action boundary
Focused schema, property, persistence, migration, and signed-route matrix
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/journey-authoring.test.ts tests/unit/domain-schemas.test.ts --project property tests/property/journey-authoring.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/journey-authoring.test.ts tests/integration/domain-migrations.test.ts apps/web/tests/integration/journey-routes.test.ts

commands.name: Causal-link locator exposed repeated checkpoint evidence
commands.expectedFailure: Two scenarios passed; the teacher assertion used a strict single-element locator even though the same checkpoint correctly appeared once for each of two linked fictional fields

commands.name: Teacher, immutable student edit, and required-visibility browser journeys
Evidence traceability required the JRN-02 manifest
commands.expectedFailure: The evidence checker reported the five new unit, property, integration, route, and Gherkin files as orphans before this manifest referenced them

commands.name: Evidence inventory rejected stale manifest and proof totals
commands.expectedFailure: The repository evidence regression still expected twelve manifests and 122 proof files after JRN-02 added the thirteenth manifest and ten new proof paths

commands.name: Evidence index includes the named-journey manifest and proofs
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts && pnpm evidence:check

commands.name: Complete gate rejected legacy placeholder journey fixtures
commands.expectedFailure: All static gates, the production build, 92 unit checks, and 32 properties passed; three older PostgreSQL integration cases still inserted empty placeholder journeys after migration 0009 began requiring real scoped prerequisites

commands.name: Existing run fixtures use complete named journeys
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/synthetic-data.test.ts tests/integration/test-authorization.test.ts

commands.name: Standalone browser follow-up used a separate cookie context
commands.expectedFailure: All 216 non-browser checks and 34 production BDD scenarios passed; the final zero-persistence GET returned 401 because Playwright's separate request context did not retain the standalone browser session cookie

commands.name: Workspace credential-pattern scan
commands.command: rg -P with token-boundary secret-key and private-key patterns, excluding dependencies, build output, artifacts, and images; no matches is success

commands.name: Unreferenced named-journey screenshots
commands.expectedFailure: The evidence harness found both source-bound JRN-02 screenshots before the manifest referenced them

commands.name: Clean-checkout verification on Ubuntu and Windows at the implementation source commit
Complete repository verification with finalized evidence

scenarios.scenario: The officer creates a runnable teacher journey with a causal chain
A student journey edit appends immutable version history
Required visibility cannot be omitted from a runnable journey

assets.altText: Desktop named journey editor after saving teacher version 1, showing the current fictional classroom rule, active authority, fictional teacher, bounded actions, required visibility, causal links, and immutable history.
assets.caption: The optimized-production teacher view links the human-confirmed rule through two fictional fields to the required assignment-published checkpoint and preserves saved version 1.

assets.altText: Narrow named journey editor after saving student version 2, showing the fictional student, required submission visibility, causal links, and immutable version 2 followed by preserved version 1.
assets.caption: The narrow optimized-production view keeps the student journey readable while proving that an edit appends version 2 without replacing version 1.

proofs.description: Runnable shape, complete causal-link examples, invalid field/checkpoint/action combinations, freezing, and lineage
Seeded PROP-25 generated causal-link completeness and immutable append proof
Permissions, current prerequisites, agreement isolation, immutable and concurrent appends, dynamic reassessment, audit, and PostgreSQL authority enforcement
Signed create/list, bounded prerequisite rejection, forged role denial, causal response, and stale append HTTP proof
Teacher authoring, immutable student edit, and required-visibility refusal through real browser and API boundaries
Playwright prerequisite, server-response, causal-link, immutable-history, zero-persistence, and visual-capture assertions
Strict draft builder, current prerequisite assessment, causal views, scoped history, services, and in-memory/PostgreSQL repositories
Journey, test-field, checkpoint, and step schemas with complete required-causal-link invariants
Scoped append-only lineage, current requirement and authorization, action complement, persona field, and human-author database guards
Migration inventory, stable checksum, cross-scope foreign keys, runnable journey references, and immutable run linkage
Existing canary persistence coverage now seeds a complete scoped journey instead of bypassing the named-journey contract
Existing direct run-queue database gates now reference a complete current named journey
Direct-language role, goal, scope, visibility, causal-chain, version-history, and honest future-state editor
Signed URL-scoped journey create/list boundary with private no-store responses
Curated JRN-02 red/green record, claim boundary, source-bound visual plan, and limitations

knownLimitations: JRN-02 defines and versions journey specifications only; JRN-03 owns deterministic replay and RUN-04 owns model-assisted repair.
RUNNABLE means the current authoring prerequisites are complete; it does not mean a test ran, passed, found no issue, established safety, or established compliance.
The editor currently selects one current human-confirmed executable rule for each default teacher or student journey, while the domain supports multiple linked requirements.
Agreement change impact and journey re-review remain the explicitly deferred FR-015 follow-on in AGR-03.
All browser evidence uses controlled fictional accounts, reserved .invalid addresses, and the private fixture runtime.
